mechanism_brief: Polyphenolic flavonol that inhibits NF-kB and stabilizes mast cells, reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and improving endothelial function.
mechanism_detailed: Acts as a direct antioxidant, modulates NF-kB, Nrf2 and AMPK pathways, inhibits xanthine oxidase and stabilizes mast cells reducing histamine release. Improves endothelial nitric oxide bioavailability.

dose_range.timing: with fatty meal, divided into 2 doses

forms_bioavailability.note: low oral bioavailability (~2%); phytosome forms increase absorption 20x

evidence_notes: RCTs in hypertension and metabolic syndrome; modest meta-analyses.

mechanism_brief: Long-chain polyunsaturated fatty acids that give rise to resolvins and protectins, resolving inflammation and improving membrane fluidity.
mechanism_detailed: EPA and DHA compete with arachidonic acid for COX/LOX, generating anti-inflammatory series-3 eicosanoids and pro-resolving mediators (E/D resolvins, protectins, maresins). Activate GPR120, reduce hepatic triglycerides via decreased SREBP-1c and increased PPAR-alpha beta-oxidation.

snp_interactions.effect: minor allele reduces conversion of ALA to EPA/DHA; requires direct dietary intake

forms_bioavailability.note: rTG improves absorption ~70% vs EE; verify oxidation index TOTOX <19

contraindications: doses >3 g/day with anticoagulants without supervision
evidence_notes: Robust meta-analyses in triglycerides, CV events (REDUCE-IT with icosapent), and NAFLD.

mechanism_brief: Alkaloid that activates AMPK and modulates microbiota, reducing glycemia and lipids with efficacy comparable to metformin in meta-analyses.
mechanism_detailed: Activates hepatic and muscle AMPK, mildly inhibits mitochondrial complex I (similar to metformin), increases LDL receptor expression via mRNA stabilization (statin-independent mechanism), and restructures microbiota toward butyrate-producing profiles.

dose_range.timing: divided into 2-3 doses with main meals

forms_bioavailability.preferred: berberine HCl with silymarin or berberine phytosome
forms_bioavailability.note: co-administration with silymarin increases bioavailability via P-gp inhibition

evidence_notes: Consistent meta-analyses in HbA1c, LDL and NAFLD; effect comparable to metformin in some endpoints.
key_references: Dong 2012, Evid Based Complement Alternat Med

mechanism_brief: Polyphenol that inhibits NF-kB, activates Nrf2 and modulates TLR4, reducing hepatic and systemic inflammation.
mechanism_detailed: Blocks NF-kB nuclear translocation, activates Nrf2/ARE pathway increasing endogenous antioxidants, inhibits STAT3 and JAK, reduces hepatic fibrogenesis via HSC inhibition. Meriva form (phosphatidylcholine phytosome) increases bioavailability 29x.

dose_range.timing: with fatty meal, divided into 2 doses

forms_bioavailability.poor: pure curcumin without piperine or lipid vehicle
forms_bioavailability.note: standard curcumin has bioavailability <1%; Meriva 29x, Theracurmin 27x

evidence_notes: Multiple RCTs in NAFLD, arthritis and metabolic syndrome; heterogeneity by galenic form.

mechanism_brief: Stilbene that activates SIRT1 and AMPK, mimicking caloric restriction effects on mitochondrial metabolism.
mechanism_detailed: Allosteric activator of SIRT1, deacetylates PGC-1alpha promoting mitochondrial biogenesis. Indirectly activates AMPK, improves hepatic insulin sensitivity and endothelial function via increased eNOS.

forms_bioavailability.note: oral bioavailability ~1%; rapidly metabolized to glucuronides

evidence_notes: Heterogeneous RCTs; positive signal in insulin sensitivity and endothelial function, weaker in mortality.

mechanism_brief: Isothiocyanate derived from glucoraphanin that activates Nrf2, the master regulator of the endogenous antioxidant response.
mechanism_detailed: Covalently modifies KEAP1 releasing Nrf2, which translocates to the nucleus and activates ~200 cytoprotective genes (GSH synthase, NQO1, HO-1, glutathione-S-transferases). Reduces hepatic glucose production in T2DM (Axelsson 2017 Science Transl Med).

snp_interactions.effect: homozygous deletion increases sulforaphane retention and efficacy

dose_range.timing: fasting or with light meal; avoid prolonged cooking of broccoli

forms_bioavailability.preferred: fresh broccoli sprouts (3-4 days) or sulforaphane stabilized with active myrosinase
forms_bioavailability.note: adding ground mustard to cooked broccoli restores conversion to sulforaphane

evidence_notes: Small RCT in T2DM with reduction in hepatic glucose; robust mechanistic evidence.

mechanism_brief: Secosteroid prohormone that after 25- and 1-alpha-hydroxylation activates VDR, modulating >1000 immune and metabolic genes.
mechanism_detailed: 1,25(OH)2D3 binds VDR/RXR regulating transcription of immune differentiation genes (regulatory T cells), calcium homeostasis, and insulin sensitivity. Deficiency (<30 ng/mL) is associated with insulin resistance, beta-cell dysfunction and inflammatory Th17 excess.

snp_interactions.effect: modulates vitamin D transport; carriers require higher doses

dose_range.timing: with fatty meal; adjust by 25(OH)D target 40-60 ng/mL

forms_bioavailability.preferred: D3 (cholecalciferol) in MCT or olive oil
forms_bioavailability.note: co-administer with K2-MK7 (90-180 mcg) to direct calcium to bone and not plaque

contraindications: calcium oxalate kidney stones without supervision
evidence_notes: Consensus in deficiency; mixed RCTs for CV prevention but robust in all-cause mortality (Bjelakovic 2014 Cochrane).
key_references: Pittas 2019, N Engl J Med (D2d trial)

mechanism_brief: Cofactor of >300 enzymes; its chronic deficit underlies insulin resistance, hypertension and neuromuscular hyperexcitability.
mechanism_detailed: ATP cofactor (Mg-ATP is the active form), required for insulin receptor tyrosine kinase, calcium channels and Na/K pump. Bisglycinate avoids the laxative effect of oxide/citrate and provides glycine (GABAergic substrate) favoring autonomic flexibility.

forms_bioavailability.note: assess erythrocyte Mg, not serum (poorly sensitive)

evidence_notes: Consistent meta-analyses in HbA1c, blood pressure and sleep quality.

mechanism_brief: Trace element structural to >300 enzymes; critical cofactor for insulin synthesis, storage and secretion.
mechanism_detailed: Component of hexameric insulin crystals in beta-cell granules (ZnT8 transporter). Cofactor of cytoplasmic superoxide dismutase, metallothioneins and thymulin. Deficiency impairs Th1 immunity and intestinal barrier healing.

snp_interactions.effect: risk allele reduces efficiency of ZnT8 transporter in beta cell

dose_range.timing: with meal; separate 2h from coffee, calcium and iron

forms_bioavailability.note: sustained doses >40 mg/day require 1-2 mg copper to avoid cupric deficiency

evidence_notes: Meta-analyses in glycemia and beta-cell function; strong evidence in immunity.
key_references: Capdor 2013, J Trace Elem Med Biol

mechanism_brief: Trace element that potentiates insulin receptor signaling via chromodulin, improving muscle glucose uptake.
mechanism_detailed: Binds apochromodulin forming holochromodulin, which activates insulin receptor tyrosine kinase and increases GLUT4 translocation in muscle. Modest evidence in carbohydrate cravings in atypical depression.

forms_bioavailability.note: picolinate crosses the cell membrane better

evidence_notes: Heterogeneous meta-analyses; modest effect on HbA1c, larger on cravings.

mechanism_brief: Component of selenoproteins (glutathione peroxidases, thioredoxin reductases) essential for antioxidant defense and T4 to T3 conversion.
mechanism_detailed: Incorporated as selenocysteine into 25 human selenoproteins. GPx1-4 neutralize peroxides, TrxR regenerates thioredoxin, DIO1-3 regulate thyroid hormones. Narrow therapeutic range: both deficit and excess are harmful (U-shaped curve).

snp_interactions.effect: modulates antioxidant efficiency; variable dietary response

forms_bioavailability.note: 2-3 Brazil nuts provide therapeutic dose; avoid supplementation if diet already includes them

contraindications: chronic overdose (selenosis: hair loss, brittle nails)
increased T2DM risk with already high selenium in soil
evidence_notes: Consensus in deficiency and autoimmune thyroiditis; mixed evidence in cancer (SELECT null).

mechanism_brief: Electron carrier between complexes I/II and III of the respiratory chain; endogenous lipophilic antioxidant that decreases with age and statins.
mechanism_detailed: Ubiquinol (reduced form) donates electrons to cytochrome c oxidase, stabilizes mitochondrial membranes, regenerates oxidized alpha-tocopherol. Statins block its synthesis by inhibiting HMG-CoA reductase (common pathway with cholesterol).

forms_bioavailability.preferred: ubiquinol (reduced form) in oil, especially >50 years old
forms_bioavailability.note: ubiquinol 3-8x more bioavailable than ubiquinone in older adults

contraindications: anticoagulants (antagonizes warfarin due to structural similarity with K2)
evidence_notes: Q-SYMBIO RCT positive in heart failure; favorable meta-analysis in statin myalgia.

mechanism_brief: Pyruvate dehydrogenase cofactor and universal amphipathic antioxidant; improves insulin sensitivity and diabetic neuropathy.
mechanism_detailed: Regenerates glutathione, vitamin C and E; chelates heavy metals; activates muscle AMPK and increases GLUT4 translocation. R-form (natural) is more active than S. Solid evidence in diabetic neuropathy (ALADIN, SYDNEY studies).

forms_bioavailability.preferred: stabilized R-ALA (Na-RALA) or racemic ALA with light meal
forms_bioavailability.note: R-form has shorter half-life but greater biological activity

evidence_notes: Positive meta-analysis in symptomatic diabetic neuropathy and HOMA-IR.

mechanism_brief: Glutathione precursor and direct thiol donor; canonical hepatoprotector and modulator of prefrontal glutamate.
mechanism_detailed: Provides cysteine, rate-limiting substrate for GSH synthesis. Reduces disulfides, modulates mGluR3 receptor rebalancing extracellular glutamate (effect on rumination and compulsive behaviors). Gold standard in acetaminophen poisoning.

forms_bioavailability.preferred: pure NAC in enteric capsule or effervescent NAC
forms_bioavailability.note: oral bioavailability 6-10%; sufficient for systemic effect

evidence_notes: Evidence A for paracetamol poisoning; B for NAFLD and OCD/trichotillomania.

mechanism_brief: Obligate transporter of long-chain fatty acids into the mitochondrial matrix via the CPT1/CPT2 system.
mechanism_detailed: Carnitine palmitoyltransferase I (CPT1) conjugates acyl-CoA with carnitine forming acyl-carnitine, which crosses the mitochondrial membrane via CACT. CPT2 regenerates intramitochondrial acyl-CoA for beta-oxidation. Deficiency causes myopathy and hepatic dysfunction. Microbiota converts carnitine to TMAO (CV relevance in rapid metabolizers).

forms_bioavailability.note: oral bioavailability 15-20%; propionyl-L-carnitine preferred in claudication

contraindications: history of elevated TMAO + established CV disease
evidence_notes: Positive meta-analysis in angina and fatigue; TMAO signal requires individualization.

mechanism_brief: Non-proteinogenic sulfonated amino acid with osmoregulatory functions, biliary conjugation and GABAergic modulation.
mechanism_detailed: Conjugates bile acids (taurocholate, taurochenodeoxycholate) increasing fat solubilization. Positive modulator of GABA-A and glycine receptors, stabilizes excitable membranes. Improves mitochondrial function and insulin sensitivity in animal and human models.

forms_bioavailability.preferred: free taurine in powder or capsule
forms_bioavailability.note: highly bioavailable; rapid renal excretion justifies divided doses

evidence_notes: RCTs in hypertension and prediabetes; recent longevity trial (Singh 2023 Science).

mechanism_brief: Inhibitory amino acid in CNS (glycinergic receptor and NMDA co-agonist), precursor of glutathione and collagen; improves sleep quality.
mechanism_detailed: Agonist of glycinergic receptor in brainstem (mild hypothermia inducing deep sleep), obligate co-agonist at NMDA site. Provides the alpha-carbon for GSH synthesis and is 33% of collagen. Combined with NAC restores GSH in aging (GlyNAC).

dose_range.timing: 30-60 min before bed for sleep; divide into doses with meals for GSH

forms_bioavailability.note: very safe; doses up to 90 g/day investigated in schizophrenia

evidence_notes: GlyNAC RCT in aging (Kumar 2021); solid evidence in sleep and insulin sensitivity.

mechanism_brief: Methyl donor that remethylates homocysteine to methionine via hepatic BHMT; cellular osmolyte and lipotropic metabolite.
mechanism_detailed: Substrate of betaine-homocysteine methyltransferase (BHMT), parallel to the 5-MTHF/B12 pathway. Reduces plasma homocysteine and provides methyl groups for phosphatidylcholine (PEMT), critical for VLDL and hepatic triglyceride export. Choline/betaine deficiency is a causal driver of NAFLD.

snp_interactions.effect: TT reduces 5-MTHF availability; betaine provides alternative remethylation pathway
risk alleles increase choline/betaine requirement to avoid NAFLD

forms_bioavailability.poor: betaine HCl at high doses (collateral gastric acidity)
forms_bioavailability.note: doses >6 g/day may raise LDL in some individuals; monitor

evidence_notes: FDA-approved for homocystinuria; consistent evidence in homocysteine and NAFLD.

mechanism_brief: Active form of B12, cofactor of methionine synthase and methylmalonyl-CoA mutase; critical for methylation and myelination.
mechanism_detailed: Cofactor of MTR (remethylates homocysteine to methionine) and MUT (conversion of methylmalonyl-CoA to succinyl-CoA). Deficiency raises homocysteine and MMA, causes neuropathy and megaloblastic anemia. Metformin, PPIs and veganism are common causes of deficiency.

snp_interactions.effect: A66G reduces regeneration of active cobalamin; requires sustained intake

forms_bioavailability.preferred: methylcobalamin or hydroxocobalamin (sublingual or IM in deficiency)
forms_bioavailability.poor: cyanocobalamin (requires demethylation) — not optimal in smokers
forms_bioavailability.note: in confirmed deficiency, initiate IM hydroxocobalamin weekly × 4-8 weeks

evidence_notes: Solid clinical consensus for deficiency; robust meta-analyses in homocysteine.

mechanism_brief: Active form of folate that crosses the blood-brain barrier; donates the methyl for homocysteine remethylation and monoamine synthesis.
mechanism_detailed: Direct substrate of methionine synthase (together with B12). Avoids the bottleneck of MTHFR (polymorphic enzyme) that converts 5,10-methylene-THF into 5-MTHF. In C677T TT carriers (activity reduced ~70%), 5-MTHF avoids accumulation of unmetabolized folic acid (UMFA).

snp_interactions.effect: C677T (TT reduces 70% activity; CT ~35%); 5-MTHF bypasses the enzyme

forms_bioavailability.poor: synthetic folic acid in MTHFR carriers (UMFA accumulation)
forms_bioavailability.note: in pregnancy with MTHFR variant, 5-MTHF preferable to folic acid

contraindications: untreated B12 deficiency (masks anemia but not neuropathy)
evidence_notes: Solid evidence in hyperhomocysteinemia, neural tube defects and depression with MTHFR variant.

mechanism_brief: Active coenzyme form of B6, cofactor of >140 enzymes including transaminases, decarboxylases and cystathionine beta-synthase.
mechanism_detailed: Cofactor of CBS (transsulfuration of homocysteine to cystathionine), GAD (GABA synthesis), AADC (dopamine/serotonin), ALAS (heme). Hepatically activated form that avoids bottleneck in individuals with pyridoxal kinase polymorphisms. Chronic excess of non-phosphorylated pyridoxine causes sensory neuropathy.

forms_bioavailability.poor: pyridoxine HCl at sustained high doses (neuropathic risk)
forms_bioavailability.note: long-term safety limit: 50-100 mg/day; avoid sustained massive doses

contraindications: L-dopa (P5P potentiates peripheral decarboxylation — separate or avoid in Parkinson's without carbidopa)
evidence_notes: Consistent evidence in homocysteine, premenstrual syndrome and peripheral neuropathy.

mechanism_brief: Flavonolignan complex (silibinin main) that stabilizes hepatocyte membrane, inhibits TGF-beta and regenerates hepatic glutathione.
mechanism_detailed: Silibinin stabilizes hepatocyte membrane blocking toxin entry (amanitin), activates ribosomal polymerase I stimulating regeneration, inhibits hepatic stellate cell activation (antifibrotic via TGF-beta), and co-administered with berberine improves bioavailability (inhibits P-gp).

forms_bioavailability.note: phytosome increases absorption ~10x

evidence_notes: Meta-analyses with positive signal in NAFLD liver enzymes; strong evidence in Amanita poisoning.

mechanism_brief: Viscous soluble fiber with high gelling capacity that sequesters bile acids and slows glycemic absorption.
mechanism_detailed: Forms viscous gel in small intestine, traps bile acids (increasing excretion and forcing the liver to synthesize more at the expense of hepatic cholesterol), reduces postprandial glycemic peak, increases satiety through gastric distension. Reduces LDL ~7% at doses of 10 g/day (FDA-approved claim).

dose_range.timing: 15-30 min before main meals, with 300 mL water

forms_bioavailability.note: start with 3-5 g/day and titrate up to avoid bloating; separate 2h from medication

evidence_notes: FDA health claim for cholesterol reduction; robust meta-analyses in LDL and glycemia.

mechanism_brief: Prebiotic fructan fermented by bifidobacteria and Faecalibacterium producing butyrate and other short-chain fatty acids.
mechanism_detailed: Non-digestible in small intestine, fermented in colon by beneficial genera (Bifidobacterium, Faecalibacterium prausnitzii, Akkermansia indirectly). Butyrate feeds colonocytes, reinforces tight junctions (occludin, ZO-1) reducing circulating LPS and metabolic endotoxemia. Stimulates GLP-1 and PYY improving satiety.

dose_range.timing: divided into 2 doses with meals; gradual introduction

forms_bioavailability.preferred: chicory inulin with mixed DP (degree of polymerization)
forms_bioavailability.poor: pure FOS at high initial doses (severe bloating)
forms_bioavailability.note: titrate from 2 g/day for microbiota adaptation; avoid in SIBO/fermenter IBS

evidence_notes: Positive meta-analysis in systemic inflammation and glycemia; heterogeneity by individual microbial substrate.

mechanism_brief: Viscous soluble fiber that sequesters bile acids and reduces LDL; FDA and EFSA claim approved at 3 g/day.
mechanism_detailed: Forms viscous gel that traps bile acids (increased fecal excretion), inducing the liver to synthesize new bile acids from hepatic cholesterol, reducing circulating LDL. Slows gastric emptying and glucose absorption. Partially fermented to butyrate in colon.

forms_bioavailability.preferred: whole oat bran or standardized concentrate >70% beta-glucan
forms_bioavailability.note: 3 g/day is the validated threshold for LDL claim; high molecular weight preserves viscosity

contraindications: uncontrolled celiac disease (cross-contamination — use certified gluten-free oats)
evidence_notes: FDA health claim and EFSA approved; robust meta-analysis (Whitehead 2014 Am J Clin Nutr) with LDL reduction ~0.25 mmol/L.
